title: Smoke-Free Day for a Healthier Way
story: Every May 31, people around the world celebrate World No Tobacco Day by spreading the message that smoking is harmful to our health. Schools teach kids about making healthy choices, while doctors and nurses share facts about how tobacco hurts our lungs and heart. Many communities organize fun runs, bike rides, and outdoor activities to show how great it feels to breathe clean air and stay active.
activity_question: What do communities often organize on World No Tobacco Day to promote healthy living?
follow_up_question: What healthy activities do you enjoy that help keep your lungs strong?

title: Sweet Coconut Celebration Day
story: Coconut lovers unite every May 31 for National Macaroon Day! These chewy, sweet treats made with shredded coconut are totally different from fancy French macarons. People celebrate by baking homemade macaroons in their kitchens, buying them from bakeries, or trying wild new flavors like chocolate-dipped or almond varieties. Some families make it a fun tradition to see who can eat the most without getting coconut stuck in their teeth! Bakeries often create special macaroon displays, and social media fills with photos of these bumpy, golden treats. It's the perfect excuse to indulge in something sweet and coconutty.
activity_question: What ingredient makes macaroons chewy and sweet?
bonus_fact: Macaroons and macarons are completely different cookies with different recipes and origins!
